channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [SHILCTECH] Shilchar Technologies Ltd Appoints Aatman Alay Shah as Whole Time Director and Arvind Nopany as Non-Executive Independent Director
item.description:
Shilchar Technologies Ltd has appointed Mr. Aatman Alay Shah as Whole Time Director and Mr. Arvind Nopany as Non-Executive Independent Director. The appointments were approved by the Shareholders of the Company through Postal Ballot Notice dated 02 February, 2026 and became effective on 06 March, 2026. The appointments are for a term of 5 consecutive years. Mr. Aatman Alay Shah has extensive experience in Project Management, Marketing, and Business Development, while Mr. Arvind Nopany is the Chairman and Managing Director of the Company. Neither of the appointees is debarred from holding the office of Director by virtue of any SEBI Order or any other such authority.

  • Aatman Alay Shah appointed as Whole Time Director
  • Arvind Nopany appointed as Non-Executive Independent Director
  • Appointments approved by Shareholders through Postal Ballot Notice
  • Appointments effective from 06 March, 2026
  • Aatman Alay Shah has extensive experience in Project Management, Marketing, and Business Development

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHILCHAR TECHNOLOGIES LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [BAJAJ-AUTO] Bajaj Auto's Subsidiary Repays Euro 480 Million Loans, Terminates Agreements with Two Banks
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Bajaj Auto Ltd. has announced that its wholly owned subsidiary, Bajaj Auto International Holdings BV (BAIH BV), has prepaid outstanding dues of Euro 480 million to two banks, Citigroup Global Markets Asia Ltd. and Standard Chartered Bank. This repayment has resulted in the termination of loan agreements with these banks. BAIH BV had initially availed unsecured loans from four banks, including JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A., DBS Bank Ltd., Citigroup Global Markets Asia Ltd., and Standard Chartered Bank, amounting to Euro 666 million for its investment activities. The loans had a term of one year. BAIH BV has also made part-prepayment of Euro 215 million and Euro 54 million to JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A. and DBS Bank Ltd., respectively.

  • Bajaj Auto's subsidiary, Bajaj Auto International Holdings BV, prepaid Euro 480 million loans to two banks
  • Loan agreements with Citigroup Global Markets Asia Ltd. and Standard Chartered Bank have been terminated
  • Initial loans amounted to Euro 666 million from four banks for investment activities
  • Part-prepayment of Euro 215 million and Euro 54 million made to JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A. and DBS Bank Ltd.
  • Loans had a term of one year

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BAJAJ AUTO LTD. | Category: Joint Ventures

item.title: [BALUFORGE] Balu Forge Industries Allots 20.6 Lakh Equity Shares Upon Convertible Warrants Exercise
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Balu Forge Industries Ltd. has allotted 20,60,000 equity shares at an issue price of Rs. 360 (including a premium of Rs. 350 per share) to the share warrant holders who exercised their option available with the Convertible Warrants. The allotment was approved by the Board of Directors on March 6, 2026, and the shares have a face value of Rs. 10 each.

  • Balu Forge Industries allotted 20,60,000 equity shares at Rs. 360 per share.
  • The allotment was made to share warrant holders who exercised their option available with the Convertible Warrants.
  • The shares have a face value of Rs. 10 each.
  • The allotment was approved by the Board of Directors on March 6, 2026.
  • The allotment was made in accordance with SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Balu Forge Industries Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [WABAG] VA Tech Wabag Ltd Receives GST Demand Order for Excess ITC Claim in FY 2019-20
item.description:
VA Tech Wabag Ltd has received a GST demand order from the Office of the Deputy Commissioner, GST Commissionerate, Uttar Pradesh, alleging excess ITC claim for the FY 2019-20. The demand order is passed along with interest and penalty under Section 74 of the CGST Act. The company will be pursuing appropriate legal remedies, which includes filing an appeal with the appropriate Authority against the said order.

  • VA Tech Wabag Ltd received a GST demand order for excess ITC claim in FY 2019-20
  • The demand order amounts to INR 43,73,666/- including basic tax, interest, and penalty
  • The company will pursue legal remedies, including filing an appeal with the appropriate Authority

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: VA TECH WABAG LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [BIJHANS] Bijoy Hans Ltd. to Change Name and Withdraw New Name Proposal
item.description:
Bijoy Hans Ltd. has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on 11/03/2026 to consider and approve several proposals. The company plans to withdraw the new name 'Arvaya Healthtech Limited' due to revised strategic business decisions. Additionally, the Board will consider changing the existing name to 'Arvaya Healthcare Limited' or 'Arvaya Healthcare and Wellness Limited' to better reflect the company's evolving business activities, vision, and long-term objectives. The proposed name change will be subject to approval from the Registrar of Companies and other applicable authorities.

  • Bijoy Hans Ltd. to withdraw the new name 'Arvaya Healthtech Limited'
  • Company to consider changing the existing name to 'Arvaya Healthcare Limited' or 'Arvaya Healthcare and Wellness Limited'
  • Name change intended to reflect the company's evolving business activities, vision, and long-term objectives
  • Board meeting scheduled on 11/03/2026 to consider and approve the proposals

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BIJOY HANS LTD. | Category: Business Update

item.title: [GENUSPOWER] Genus Power Infrastructures Ltd: Chief Human Resources Officer, Mr. Janesh Kumar, Resigns
item.description:
Genus Power Infrastructures Ltd has announced the resignation of Mr. Janesh Kumar from the post of Chief Human Resources Officer with effect from close of business hours of March 06, 2026 due to personal reasons. The resignation was intimated to the company through a resignation letter. Mr. Janesh Kumar was appointed as the Chief Human Resources Officer and served the company till his resignation.

  • Mr. Janesh Kumar has resigned from the post of Chief Human Resources Officer at Genus Power Infrastructures Ltd.
  • The resignation is effective from close of business hours of March 06, 2026.
  • The resignation was intimated to the company through a formal letter.
  • Mr. Janesh Kumar was appointed as the Chief Human Resources Officer and served the company till his resignation.
  • The resignation is in compliance with Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GENUS POWER INFRASTRUCTURES LTD.-$ | Category: Management Change

item.title: [VARDMNPOLY] Vardhman Polytex Ltd Postpones Board Meeting to Consider Fund Raising
item.description:
Vardhman Polytex Ltd, a company listed on the BSE, has informed that the board meeting scheduled for March 6, 2026, has been postponed and rescheduled to March 11, 2026. The meeting will consider and approve fund raising through debt, debentures, equity shares, or any other instrument or security. The fund raising could be through a preferential issue or any other permissible mode and/or combination. The approvals required include those of the members and other statutory/regulatory approvals. The trading window for dealing in securities of the company will reopen after 48 hours from the declaration of the outcome of the board meeting.

  • Vardhman Polytex Ltd board meeting postponed to March 11, 2026
  • Meeting to consider and approve fund raising through debt or equity
  • Fund raising could be through preferential issue or other permissible modes
  • Approval of members and statutory/regulatory bodies required
  • Trading window to reopen after 48 hours from board meeting outcome

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: VARDHMAN POLYTEX LTD. | Category: Investment

item.title: [VELHO] Madras High Court Grants Stay on Velan Hotels' Audi Car Entry Tax Recovery
item.description:
Velan Hotels Ltd has received an order from the Hon'ble Madras High Court in relation to the demand of payment of Entry Tax on an Audi Car imported in 2006, along with Penalty and Interest. The High Court has disposed of the writ petition and passed an order allowing the Company to file an appeal before the Appellate Authority within 30 days from the date of the order. The recovery of the Entry Tax, Penalty and Interest has been kept in abeyance for 60 days from the order, and the Company is required to secure an appropriate order from the Appellate Authority to stay further recovery thereafter. Velan Hotels will continue to make disclosures updating material developments till the event is resolved or closed, with relevant explanations.

  • Madras High Court passes order in Velan Hotels' Entry Tax demand case
  • Company allowed to file an appeal before the Appellate Authority within 30 days
  • Recovery of Entry Tax, Penalty and Interest kept in abeyance for 60 days
  • Velan Hotels required to secure a stay order from the Appellate Authority
  • Regular disclosures to be made updating material developments till the event is resolved

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: VELAN HOTELS LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [5PAISA] 5paisa Capital Ltd. to raise funds through Rights Issue of up to INR 4,750.00 million
item.description:
5paisa Capital Ltd. has informed BSE that the Board of Directors will meet on March 11, 2026, to consider and approve various matters related to raising funds through a rights issue. The Issue, which is expected to raise an amount not exceeding INR 4,750.00 million, will be conducted in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013, SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, and other applicable laws. The Board will discuss and decide on specific terms of the Issue, including the Issue price, rights entitlement ratio, record date, and timing of the Issue.

  • 5paisa Capital Ltd. to raise funds through Rights Issue
  • The Issue is expected to raise up to INR 4,750.00 million
  • The Board meeting is scheduled for March 11, 2026, to discuss and approve various matters related to the Issue
  • The Issue will be conducted in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013, SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, and other applicable laws
  • Specific terms of the Issue, including Issue price, rights entitlement ratio, record date, and timing of the Issue will be determined by the Board

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: 5paisa Capital Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [NOVARTIND] Novartis India Receives Draft Letter of Offer from Axis Capital Limited for Open Offer
item.description:
Novartis India Ltd has received a draft letter of offer dated March 05, 2026 from Axis Capital Limited, the manager to the open offer. This comes after the public announcement on February 19, 2026 and the detailed public statement received on February 26, 2026 regarding the open offer made by WaveRise Investments Limited, ChrysCapital Fund X, Two Infinity Partners, and their respective associates. The company has received the draft letter for information and record purposes.

  • Novartis India has received a draft letter of offer from Axis Capital Limited.
  • The draft letter is related to the open offer made by WaveRise Investments Limited, ChrysCapital Fund X, Two Infinity Partners, and their respective associates.
  • The public announcement was made on February 19, 2026 and the detailed public statement was received on February 26, 2026.
  • The company has received the draft letter for information and record purposes.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NOVARTIS INDIA LTD. | Category: Deals
